title: Király's Cycle Bound Conjecture
statement_text: The number of cycles in a simple graph with $m$ edges is $O(1.4^m)$.
context_text: Király [8] conjectured that the number of cycles in a graph with $m$ edges is $O(1.4^m)$. This is stated as the primary motivation in the introduction, and Theorem 1 of this paper proves the conjecture by establishing the stronger bound $O(\gamma^m)$ with $\gamma \approx 1.384$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture; Theorem 1 establishes the stronger bound $O(\gamma^m)$ where $\gamma \approx 1.384 < 1.4$.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. Publication year of Király [8] not determinable from extracted text.
paper_title: Bounding the number of cycles in a graph in terms of its degree sequence

title: Király's 4-Regular Graph Conjecture
statement_text: A $4$-regular simple graph with $m$ edges has at most $1.99^{m/2}$ cycles.
context_text: Király [8] conjectured that 4-regular graphs have at most $1.99^{m/2}$ cycles; this paper also improves on Király's earlier result of $O((\sqrt{2}-\varepsilon)^m)$ for graphs of average degree at most four. Theorem 3 proves this conjecture with the stronger bound $O(1.365^m)$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture; Theorem 3 establishes the stronger bound $O(1.365^m)$ for simple graphs with $m$ edges and average degree $4$.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. Publication year of Király [8] not determinable from extracted text.
paper_title: Bounding the number of cycles in a graph in terms of its degree sequence

title: Arman–Tsaturian Conjecture 6.1
statement_text: A simple graph with $n$ vertices and average degree $d$ has at most $\left(1 + O\!\left(\frac{\log d}{d}\right)\right)^n \cdot \left(\frac{d}{e}\right)^n$ cycles.
context_text: Conjecture 6.1 of Arman and Tsaturian [2] predicts an asymptotic upper bound on the number of cycles in graphs with superconstant average degree. Theorem 5 of this paper proves the conjecture, and the bound is shown to be asymptotically tight by considering random graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 6.1 of Arman and Tsaturian; the bound is asymptotically tight as witnessed by random graphs [6].
notes: PDF source — exact verbatim statement of the conjecture is not reproduced in the paper; statement inferred from Theorem 5, which the authors explicitly say proves it. Math may be garbled.
paper_title: Bounding the number of cycles in a graph in terms of its degree sequence

title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: Every simple graph with $m$ edges has at most $O(\kappa_1^m)$ cycles, where $\kappa_1 = (2+2\sqrt{2})^{1/5} \approx 1.3701$.
context_text: The Arman–Tsaturian construction [2] gives simple graphs with $\Omega(\kappa_1^m)$ cycles, which is the best known lower bound. The authors verified by computer several similar constructions and could not find a better one, leading them to conjecture that this construction is optimal. Remark 9 notes that any counterexample would necessarily have average degree in the range $(4.24, 7.18)$.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled; value of $\kappa_1$ reconstructed from surrounding prose.
paper_title: Bounding the number of cycles in a graph in terms of its degree sequence
